warnings: recipe_defect: .claude/commands/run-analyzer.md Step 1 specifies `bq --format=json query --use_legacy_sql=false --max_rows=10000 --project_id="$BQ_PROJECT" "$SQL"`; two issues confirmed again on this run: (1) `--max_rows=10000` is not a valid `bq query` flag (only `head` accepts it); (2) passing SQL as a positional arg fails when the SQL contains unicode box-drawing characters (`─` U+2500) used in this project's sql/ headers, because bq's flag parser treats them as flag-like input and crashes with a Python RecursionError in _damerau_levenshtein. Workaround applied: pipe SQL via stdin (`echo "$SQL" | bq --format=json query --use_legacy_sql=false --max_rows=10000 --project_id=...`) — bq accepts --max_rows as a global head-style flag at this position but ignores it for query; the stdin path avoids the parser crash. Fix the recipe in a Phase 2 follow-up.
